rulings.Case Title: CHARLES ZETTNER et al VS. BUFFALO PUMPS INC(SUED INDIVIDULLY AND AS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Continuance Of Hrg Re Deft Joy Technologies, Inc'S Mo To Stay Or Dismiss Pltfs' 2nd Amended Complt On The Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JULY 9, 2007, LINE 4. DEFENDANT JOY TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S MOTION TO STAY OR DISMISS PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT ON THE GROUNDS OF FORUM NON CONVENIENS IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH HANSEN PROVIDES SUPPORT FOR THE MOTION, THE FACTS OF THIS CASE ARE MORE ANALOGOUS TO AMERICAN CEMWOOD CORP. V AMERICAN HOME ASSURANCE CO. (2001) 87 CAL. APP. 4TH 431, 439 AND DEFENDANT HAS NOT MADE A SUFFICIENT SHOWING UNDER AMERICAN. =(302/PJM/JN-X)

rulings.Case Title: SCOTT SMITH VS. MCCORMICK AND SCHMICK RESTAURANT CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JULY 9, 2007, LINE 7. DEFENDANT MCCORMICK AND SCHMICK RESTAURANT CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: HEARING REQUIRED. THE COURT CANNOT ADDRESS THE MERITS OF THE MOTION UNTIL "DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE RE PLAINTIFF'S SEPARATE STATEMENT" IS SUBMITTED IN THE FORMAT UTILIZED BY DEFENDANT WHEN OBJECTING TO THE DECLARATIONS AND INCLUDES A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER SETTING FORTH THE TESTIMONY AND DOCUMENT AND THE PRECISE BASIS FOR THE OBJECTION. IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFF IS A MEMBER OF A PROTECTED CLASS; THERE IS EVIDENCE OF DISCRIMINATION WITH RESPECT TO THE LEAVE (PLAINTIFF'S FACTS 8 THROUGH 16). DEFENDANT HAS ARTICULATED A LEGITIMATE, NONDISCRIMINATORY REASON FOR TERMINATING PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF HAS PROVIDED EVIDENCE OF PRETEXT BASED ON EVIDENCE THAT APPEARS TO BE ADMISSIBLE: EXHIBIT 1 CALDWELL 49, 75 - 76; EXHIBIT 26; EXHIBIT 29 GONZALES 114 - 118, 122; EXHIBIT 30 JEGHELIAN 148; EXHIBIT 31 SKEELE 191 - 192, 196 -197, 200 - 202; EXHIBIT 12 GABICA 321 AND EXHIBIT 14. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Case Title: VERONA PARTNERS, LLC et al VS. BANK OF AMERICA SECURITIES, LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JULY 9, 2007, LINE 8. DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA SECURITIES, L.L.C. AND BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS TRANSFERRED TO DEPT. 301 PER RECUSAL. MATTER TO BE HEARD ON JULY 19, 2007. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: PETER FADER VS. JOHN L GADDY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JULY 9, 2007, LINE 9. CROSS-DEFENDANTS PETER FADER AND CYNTHIA DIETZMANN'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION; THE DEMURRER FAILS TO ADDRESS ALLEGATIONS RE RETURNED CHECKS; FIVE PER CENT IS NOT UNREASONABLE ON ITS FACE; A PARTY MAY SEEK DAMAGES FOR ANY BREACH OF THE CONTRACT; THE MATERIALITY OF THE BREACH IS RELEVANT ONLY ON THE ISSUE OF EXCUSING PERFORMANCE. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION WITH LEAVE TO AMEND WITHIN TEN DAYS TO PLEAD FACTS GIVING RISE TO A CLAIM NOT EMBRACED BY THE CONTRACT AND TO DEMONSTRATE HOW CROSS-COMPLAINANT WAS INJURED BY THE OPEN DOOR. =(302/PJM/KD)

rulings.Case Title: LAYLA MANN VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER CALIFORNIA et al

rulings.Case Title: HOLLYWOOD PARK LAND COMPANY, LLC, A DELAWARE et al VS. BILL LOCKYER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Change Of Venue
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JULY 9, 2007, LINE 11. RESPONDENT BILL LOCKYER'S, TREASURER OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, Motion For Change Of Venue IS DENIED. THIS IS A VALIDATION ACTION. CALIFORNIA COMMERCE CASINO, INC. V SCHWARZNEGGER (2007) 146 CAL. APP. 4TH, 1406, 1432. UNDER C.C.P. SECTIONS 860 AND 863, THIS ACTION MUST BE BROUGHT WHERE THE PRINCIPLE OFFICE OF THE PUBLIC AGENCY IS LOCATED; HERE, THAT IS SAN DIEGO. C.C.P. SECTION 401 APPLIES WHEN THE NORMAL RULES OF VENUE ALLOW FOR TRIAL IN SACRAMENTO. UNDER C.C.P. SECTION 401(1), PLAINTIFF HAS THE RIGHT TO BRING THE ACTION IN SAN FRANCISCO, WHERE THE ATTORNEY GENERAL HAS AN OFFICE. =(302/PJM)

SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JULY 9, 2007, LINE 12. PETITIONER NORMAN VORTIGY'S Motion For Reconsideration IS DENIED. THE WISE LETTER IS NOT A STATEMENT UNDER OATH AND MAY NOT BE CONSIDERED AS EVIDENCE. THE GRANT DEED DOES NOT ADDRESS THE QUESTION OF THE ISSUANCE OF MEMBERSHIPS. MR. VORTIGY'S CONCERNS ARE APPROPRIATELY ADDRESSED BY THE OFFICE OF THE ATTORNEY GENERAL. THE TIME TO OBJECT TO THE FORM OF THE PRIOR ORDER HAS EXPIRED UNDER RULES OF COURT 391(A). THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. =(302/PJM/KD)
